title: Kerem Berkalp Çerekçi
text:
Hello! My name is Kerem Berkalp Çerekçi.
This is my personal webpage.
I am a student at Hacettepe University. This is my personal webpage for EMU430.



 Back to top

title: About Me
text:
B.S., Industrial Engineering, Hacettepe University, Turkey, 2020 - 2026.







ASCENDUM | Service Intern | May 2025 - October 2025 | ANKARA

Tracking of machine, oil sample, and component data; providing a data stream integrated with production for maintenance planning and predictive analysis.
Analysis of machine data; machine updates, maintenance, and parts management.
Review of product trees and component structures; parts support for maintenance operations.
Coordination of technician scheduling and overtime management

AYESAŞ | Production Planning and Control Intern | August 2024 - September 2024 | ANKARA

Analysis of process analysis and improvement methods; proposed solutions to issues affecting production quality.
Order-driven work study planning and lean manufacturing practices.
Optimization of bottlenecks through overtime scheduling







TOC & DES Based Simulation Model | MITAS GROUP | October 2025 – February 2025 | ANKARA

A term-end project aimed at developing a TOC- and DES-based simulation model to perform production line capacity analysis, identify the primary bottleneck, and generate improvement alternatives for capacity expansion through scenario analysis.

Rough Cut Capacity Analysis | PETLAS | February 2025 – July 2025 | KIRŞEHİR

Rough Capacity Planning (RCCP) for Tire Types: A Term Project.
Capacity planning analysis and development of an Excel macro-based algorithm to improve

Database Management Systems | HACETTEPE UNIVERSITY | February 2025 – July 2025 | ANKARA

A MySQL database project that enhances the planning efficiency of academic staff by preventing exam schedule conflicts.

Reduction In Lost Shipments | ARAS KARGO | September 2024 – January 2024 | ANKARA

A lean manufacturing project aimed at reducing lost shipments and improving warehouse efficiency.

Arat | TALAY ROBOTICS SYSTEMS | November 2020 – January 2021 | ANKARA

Teknofest Preliminary Design Report (15th), Critical Design Report (5th).





R, Quarto, Git, Python, SAP, MySQL, Excel-VBA

title: Assignment 1
text:
1 + 1

[1] 2


My first assignment has two parts.


The video I chose for this assignment is “Conversations on Data Science and Industrial Engineering” featuring Baykal Hafızoğlu and Erdi Daşdemir.
Baykal Hafızoğlu completed his bachelor’s and master’s degrees at METU in 2005 and 2007, respectively, and subsequently earned his Ph.D. from Arizona State University. The companies he has worked for include LLamasoft, Predit, and FICO, and his roles have spanned operations research, optimization, and supply chain analytics.
Mr. Baykal currently serves as Director of Platform Management at FICO in the U.S.
Hafızoğlu discusses four categories within the analytical field: -Descriptive, -Diagnostic -Predictive -Prescriptive analytics.
He notes that an industrial engineering education already covers these four areas and therefore emphasizes the value of the curriculum’s content. He points out that many data science master’s programs actually cover material that industrial engineering students have already learned.
One of his strongest arguments relates to implementation: He argues that an analytical model has no real value if it is not delivered to end users. In one of his projects at Procter & Gamble, he developed a “Product Life Cycle Management” application containing 50,000 lines of code; however, he notes that only 100 of those lines actually comprised the optimization model. The remaining code handled data processing, the user interface, and software engineering tasks. This demonstrates that making a model usable is just as important as creating it.




Open-ended Question:
Why is it important to establish a clear problem definition with measurable KPIs when starting a project? What kinds of issues might arise without this?
Answer:
Without a clear and measurable problem definition, project teams risk losing their direction and failing to communicate effectively with stakeholders. All parties involved in the process must be able to express their communication using measurable metrics (KPIs) rather than vague statements. Without this foundation, even the most advanced analytical model becomes meaningless because success cannot be measured.
Additionally, defining success criteria in advance enables teams to objectively evaluate their solutions and demonstrate the solution’s value to decision-makers. Furthermore, tracking conflicting KPIs is equally important, as improving one metric may inadvertently worsen another.
Multiple-choice Question:
Which of the following best describes prescriptive analytics?
A) Summarizing and visualizing historical data to understand what happened.
B) Identifying the root causes of a problem that has already occurred.
C) Predicting future outcomes using statistical and machine learning models.
D) Recommending specific actions to take in order to achieve a desired outcome.
Answer:
Suggesting the specific steps that need to be taken to achieve the desired outcome. It tells rule-based, analytical decision-makers exactly what steps they need to take. ( D )
